title: RPYR-0017 — ctypes `Pointer.__setitem__` narrows the written value with `.expect("int too large")`, so `p[0] = 2**64` aborts the VM where CPython masks to the C width (_ctypes/pointer.rs:690-693)
one_line_repro: import ctypes; p=ctypes.pointer(ctypes.c_int(0)); p[0]=2**64   # panic-abort (CPython masks to 0)
prior_art: Sibling of fuzzer RUSTPY-0017 (the ctypes simple-type int-narrowing class, `_ctypes/simple.rs:895/908/921` — `c_char_p(2**64)` etc.). Same `.to_*().expect("int too large")` shape, a NEW site the 0017 report did not cover. No specific RustPython issue.
found_by: ctypes-ffi-auditor (informed explore — the run's real new ctypes find; the only `.expect()` narrowing outside simple.rs)
panic-site-auditor (independently surfaced the same site — dual-found)
cpython_behavior: CPython masks the write value to the C width (assigning 2**64 to a c_int slot stores 0); it does not abort. RustPython panics.
guarded_twin: In-file inconsistency: every array/base/function ctypes write sibling uses the SILENT `.to_usize().unwrap_or(0)` variant (which fails safe), while the pointer write path uses `.expect("int too large")` (which aborts). The simple-type siblings (RUSTPY-0017) share the abort shape; the fix is the same file-sweep to a checked conversion that raises a Python error.
notes: `Pointer.__setitem__` narrows the assigned value to the pointee C width with `.to_u8()/.to_i16()/.to_i32()/.to_i64().expect("int too large")` (pointer.rs:690-693). Any Python int outside the target width panics -> VM abort (exit 101). Verified live: `p=ctypes.pointer(ctypes.c_int(0)); p[0]=2**64` -> `panicked at pointer.rs:692 'int too large'`, exit 101; CPython stores the masked value (0) and continues. This is the write-side face of RUSTPY-0017 (which is the from-int construction side in simple.rs). FIX: same as 0017 -- map the out-of-range conversion to a Python OverflowError instead of `.expect()`, ideally the same helper across simple.rs/array.rs/pointer.rs. Note the silent `.unwrap_or(0)` siblings are NOT this bug: the ctypes-ffi differential showed they fail safe (RustPython raises ValueError on NULL rather than deref garbage) -- see the informed-explore SUMMARY.
